observations.recommendation: We recommend management to comply with the required submission to COA of POs together with supporting documents within the prescribed period. Management shall ensure that the activities/documents needed prior to the execution of the contract have been conducted and completely accomplished. It shall likewise monitor the delivery of the items procured in accordance with the terms of the contract especially if time is of the essence.
observations.managementAction: The new BAC was instructed to furnish copies of Purchase Orders together with supporting documents to the Audit Team within 5 working days from issuance. However, delayed compliance due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accountable Officers monitor the expiration of their fidelity bond and apply for renewal before expiry. Also, require the Accountable Officer to be properly bonded before doing the collecting and disbursing function.  Further, submit to the audit team the confirmation letter from the Bureau of Treasury as soon as the bonds are renewed.

We recommend that management:
Adhere to the rules and regulations of the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
Send demand letters to concerned officers to enforce immediate settlement of their cash advances otherwise enforce appropriate administrative/legal sanctions.
For the rehired officer as Executive Assistant we recommend to withhold the salary and/ or other benefits due her notwithstanding the imposition of other sanctions provided under Article 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommend management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advance.
Issue demand letters to notify the concerned officials and employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If the settlement was not received, withhold their salaries for those officers/employees who are still employed. For employees who are already resigned and transferred, cause for immediate liquidation or settlement of the unliquidated cash advances.
Ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for granting cash advances in processing claims for payment thereof, particularly the certification that he/she has no outstanding cash advance.
Request for the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances balance of dead employees.

We recommend the inventory committee assess the existence and valuation of Biological Asset to establish its correct account balance. Also, conduct physical count of the reported Biological Asset and submits the Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year. Further, we recommend the Property Officer maintain a Biological Asset Property Card for easy monitoring of the condition of the Plant and Trees.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same circular.

We recommend that Accounting Office continue tracing and locating relevant documents to serve as a basis in analyzing prior years’ reconciling items and prepare necessary adjustments. Further, conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the difference.

We recommend that the management adopt and implement the guidelines in the use of post-paid plan subscriptions taking into consideration the following:
Require the accounting office to include in the DV particulars the end-user of the post-paid mobile plans and ensure that the concerned officials and employees submit the billing statements to the accounting office, including the breakdown of additional charges incurred before processing the vouchers.
Grant only to officials and employees who are department heads and consider the necessity, amount and rank in the ceiling of the postpaid mobile plan.
Stop allowing the payment of gadget cash-out or device amortization to the monthly subscription plan. Request the end-user for the refund of the gadget cash-out or device amortization on the previous billing statement.
Utilize alternative modes of communication such as the internet for long-distance communication or consider the features of the communication network of its unlimited calls for Smart to Smart/Globe to Globe phones that provide cost-saving measures.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that claims for repair has to be supported with records on repair history as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Also, instruct the Accounting Office to reconstruct the maintenance/repair record of each vehicle owned and/or operated by the municipal government.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer renew the registration of all service motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with RA No. 4136 to avoid penalties for late payment and payment of damages for third-party liabilities in case of accidents.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to support all claims for BAC honoraria with the Certified List of Successfully Completed Procurement Projects together with the Notice of Awards in accordance with Section 5.1 of Budget Circular No. 2004-5A to avoid suspension in audit. Also, submit the Certified List of Successfully Completed Procurement Projects to support the claim.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 on the payment of honoraria to BAC and TWG members. We also recommend the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that savings from the approved local budget has been approved by the Sanggunian before effecting payment chargeable to the General Fund.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A on the payment of honoraria to BAC and TWG members. We also recommend that Management grant honoraria to the BAC Members, BAC-TWG and BAC Secretariat within the limitation set forth under Item 5.3 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A. Furthermore, submit the Certified List of Successfully Completed Procurement Projects to support the claim to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the transactions to its appropriate accounts as required under Section 112 of PD No. 1445 to present fairly the balances of the account in the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and coordinate in fast tracking the implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the 20 percent Development Fund in the year the funds were appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and desired outcome from the said program or projects.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18,  Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management ensure and fast track the implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the 1% LCPC fund as required under Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.2012-120 to ensure the achievement of its intended purpose.

We recommend that management prioritize the filling up the vacant position of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) and Municipal Assessor (MA) by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving employees for the more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.

Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer under her own accountability. Moreover, she has to liquidate immediately the balances of her cash advances/withdrawals as of December 31, 2020.
Designate or appoint a disbursing officer to handle the disbursing functions pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the excess amount of PEI be refunded by the officials and employees.
observations.managementAction:
We believe, the DBM did care to go at length of specifically making 6 limitations to prepare for the possibility or as a guideline for LGU’s granting more than P5,000.00.  The LGU’s have the discretion to set the amount more than P5,000.00 as long as they will not violate the 6 limitations.  That is how we interpret it.  How could there be discretion to set the amount if it is after all subject to the P5,000.00 limitation that is specifically imposed on executive branch, legislative, judicial and GOCCs.  We believe, there is no way they go at length of making these 6 precautions or limitations because the LGUs are capable of giving more than what was given to the national agencies.

We believe the DBM did not consider it a ceiling or else they would have clarified it to avoid confusion.  They would have specifically put the limitation by putting the words “maximum amount” and “not exceeding” and deleting the words “discretion to set the amount”.

We grant the PEI based on our honest interpretation of the Budget Circular which we believe grants us the discretion based on some limitations.  It was granted based on approved budget by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.  We never had any intention of going against the Budget Circular and suffer Disallowances. We granted it in “Good Faith” and based on those reasons mentioned.

We do not believe the Circular is meant to confuse us and then lure us to give more than what is allowed and then suffer disallowances.  We interpret the Circular as it is, as a guide and we have followed it based on the spirit of our honest interpretation and out of Good Faith.

Instruct the GSO or designated Supply Officer of the LGU to ensure sufficient stock of supplies for use by management through scheduled procurement in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan for the year.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned, in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I. observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:  
Require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the submission of the required reports for the months under our audit period and for the ensuing months pursuant to Section 51 of the NGAS Manual Volume II, which are necessary to enable this Office to readily determine her accountabilities at any given time and facilitate the conduct of audit/cash examination, to wit:

a.a. The Monthly Abstract of General and Real Property Tax Collections
a.b. The Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms

b. Enforce strict compliance on the daily remittance/turn-over/deposit of collections by the collectors and Liquidating Officer by checking the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) personally daily including verification of issuance and receipt of accountable forms. No collector should be issued another booklet of accountable form unless all collections received from the issuance of previous ones are duly remitted/turned-over.
c. Maintain a Control Logbook for all accountable forms especially the Cash Tickets, such that the total accountability for accountable forms could also be readily determined at any given time.

We recommend management to instruct the Municipal Engineer to post signage /billboard at the project site and should see to it that it is in accordance with the standard form as required under COA Circular 2013-004. Moreover, the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader should be informed of the posting within ten days after award of the project as stipulated in Section 3.1 of the same circular.

We recommend that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase orders for goods procured, together with the integral parts/supporting documents thereof, within five (5) days from perfection thereof to enable the Audit Team to conduct the auditorial /legal review of these documents at the most appropriate time and manner pursuant to COA Circular No. 89-299.  Further, instruct the Inspection Officer or Inspectorate Team to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.
